extraction.one_line_summary: MILAN Jonathan won stage 2 sprint in San Salvo Marina after late crash at 3.7km split GC group, with GEOGHEGAN HART losing time.
tactical_patterns: Crash at 3.7km (just 700m before 3km rule) caught GC riders, shows positioning critical beyond traditional safe zone.
LAPEIRA Paul attacked solo for KOM jersey on debut Grand Tour, successful tactical initiative for minor jersey competition.
GANDIN Stefano swept both intermediate sprints from breakaway, suggests weak sprinter depth in main peloton on flat stages.
SINKELDAM Ramon provided leadout for GROVES Kaden, indicating UAE structured sprint train despite Giro not primary sprint focus.

rider_signals.MILAN Jonathan: positive: Grand Tour debut road stage win with powerful center-lane sprint suggests genuine top-tier sprint speed
rider_signals.GEOGHEGAN HART Tao: negative: Caught behind 3.7km crash losing GC time, poor positioning awareness on supposedly routine sprint stage
rider_signals.DEKKER David: positive: Second place finish indicates strong sprint form despite thin leadout support
rider_signals.LAPEIRA Paul: positive: Controlled breakaway and secured KOM jersey on first Grand Tour road stage, mature tactical execution
rider_signals.CAVENDISH Mark: negative: Caught in 3.7km crash and out of contention, positioning vulnerability on straightforward sprint stage
rider_signals.VLASOV Aleksandr: positive: Moved up in GC standings while others crashed, suggests strong positioning discipline in technical finales
